open_feedback_count.description:
Number of pending / fixing / addressed feedback items on the station at
the time of the call. Zero means the user slots were stamped.

stamped_user_slots.description: True when this call stamped the user review/approval slots.

station.description: The station the advance signal resolved against.

DirectionAnnotations.description:
Annotations the operator attaches when giving a design direction — pins on
the chosen archetype, an optional captured-screenshot reference, and a list
of free-text comments.

screenshot.description:
Optional reference to a captured screenshot — a `data:image/...;base64`
URL or a server-relative artifact path.

DirectionPin.description: A pin dropped on a direction preview at a relative coordinate.

note.description: The note attached to the pin.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to the preview width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to the preview height).

DirectionSelectRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /direction/:id/select` — the operator's design
decision: which archetype they chose plus optional annotations.

annotations.description: Annotations on the chosen direction — pins, screenshot ref, comments.

archetype.description:
The id of the chosen archetype (must match one of the session's
`archetypes[].id`).

DirectionSelectRequest.title: DirectionSelectRequest

archetype.description: The chosen archetype id, echoed back.

DirectionAnnotations.description:
Annotations the operator attaches when giving a design direction — pins on
the chosen archetype, an optional captured-screenshot reference, and a list
of free-text comments.

screenshot.description:
Optional reference to a captured screenshot — a `data:image/...;base64`
URL or a server-relative artifact path.

DirectionArchetype.description:
One design ARCHETYPE card in a direction session — a named design direction,
always backed by a generated preview image.

description.description: Description of the design direction this archetype represents.

image_url.description:
Generated preview-image URL for this archetype. When `image_url_light` is
also set, this is the **dark-theme** variant.

image_url_light.description:
Optional **light-theme** variant of `image_url`. When present, the
archetype is multi-theme: the view shows whichever matches the active
theme. Absent → `image_url` is theme-neutral and always shown.

DirectionPin.description: A pin dropped on a direction preview at a relative coordinate.

note.description: The note attached to the pin.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to the preview width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to the preview height).

SessionStatus.description: Runtime status spanning every interactive session type.

DirectionSessionPayload.description:
The direction-session payload (`session_type = "direction"`) — a DESIGN
DIRECTION the agent asks for: a prompt plus design archetypes (each an
image-backed direction), the chosen archetype id, and annotations.

annotations.description: Annotations attached to the chosen direction.

archetypes.description: The design archetypes to choose between.

chosen_archetype.description: The id of the archetype the operator chose, once decided.

context.description: Optional markdown preamble above the archetype cards.

prompt.description: The prompt rendered above the archetype cards.

DirectionSessionPayload.title: DirectionSessionPayload

FeedbackAnchor.description: Pin-anchor metadata for visual (pin-drop) annotations on a create request.

page_id.description: The page id the pin sits on.

viewport_height.description: Viewport height in pixels at drop time.

viewport_width.description: Viewport width in pixels at drop time.

FeedbackInlineAnchor.description: Inline text-anchor metadata for inline-comment feedback.

comment_id.description: DOM id of the saved highlight span.

content_sha.description: Content hash at save time, for drift detection.

file_path.description: Full repo-root-relative path to the anchored artifact.

location.description: Human-readable label shown in the feedback card.

paragraph.description: Zero-based paragraph index inside the reviewed artifact.

selected_text.description: The exact highlighted text the comment anchors to.

FeedbackOrigin.description:
Where a feedback item originated.

Kept in lockstep with the on-disk feedback-origin enum so a projected item
never fails the wire parse. Drives [`AuthorType`] derivation.

FeedbackResolution.description:
Routing hint for the manager's feedback resolver.

`None` on the wire means the caller has no preference and the router
defaults to `stage_revisit`.

oneOf.description: Skip the fix loop; answer the question.
Run a single fix-worker pass against the one finding.

attachment_data_url.description: Optional PNG/JPEG/WebP attachment as a data URL.

author.description:
Optional author hint (the server always stamps `user` for HTTP
submissions, so this is retained for compatibility only).

FeedbackStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of a feedback item.

`escalated` is reached when a worker-authored item's fix loop exceeds the
bolt cap without closure — a human-intervention waypoint, not a blocker.
Only `pending` and `fixing` block the checkpoint gate.

oneOf.description: A fix-worker is actively addressing it.
A fix has landed, awaiting verification.
Resolved by a reply, no code delta.
Valid but needs no code fix.
Bolt cap exceeded; awaiting a human.

file.description: Path to the committed feedback file, relative to the run dir.

AuthorType.description:
Authorship type derived from a feedback item's origin.

Human-authored feedback cannot be closed or deleted by workers; `system`
is reserved for manager-authored items (e.g. reject-loop escalation).

ClosureReply.description: A worker's plain-language reply at closure time.

FeedbackInlineAnchor.description: Inline text-anchor metadata for inline-comment feedback.

comment_id.description: DOM id of the saved highlight span.

content_sha.description: Content hash at save time, for drift detection.

file_path.description: Full repo-root-relative path to the anchored artifact.

location.description: Human-readable label shown in the feedback card.

paragraph.description: Zero-based paragraph index inside the reviewed artifact.

selected_text.description: The exact highlighted text the comment anchors to.

FeedbackIteration.description: One bolt in a finding's fix-loop history.

commit.description: Git SHA of the commit the worker produced, when one was made.

message.description: The handoff baton recorded on this transition.

FeedbackOrigin.description:
Where a feedback item originated.

Kept in lockstep with the on-disk feedback-origin enum so a projected item
never fails the wire parse. Drives [`AuthorType`] derivation.

FeedbackReply.description:
A reply on a feedback thread — answers a question, records a worker's
closure justification, or threads a short discussion.

author.description: Free-form author handle (`user`, a worker name).

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the reply was written.

FeedbackResolution.description:
Routing hint for the manager's feedback resolver.

`None` on the wire means the caller has no preference and the router
defaults to `stage_revisit`.

oneOf.description: Skip the fix loop; answer the question.
Run a single fix-worker pass against the one finding.

FeedbackScope.description: The scope a feedback item lives in.

oneOf.description: Run-scope (logged by the run-level completion review).

FeedbackSeverity.description:
Finding urgency — drives fix-worker dispatch order.

Nullable on the wire: a human-authored item has no severity until the
classifier fix-worker backfills it.

FeedbackStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of a feedback item.

`escalated` is reached when a worker-authored item's fix loop exceeds the
bolt cap without closure — a human-intervention waypoint, not a blocker.
Only `pending` and `fixing` block the checkpoint gate.

oneOf.description: A fix-worker is actively addressing it.
A fix has landed, awaiting verification.
Resolved by a reply, no code delta.
Valid but needs no code fix.
Bolt cap exceeded; awaiting a human.

IterationResult.description: The result of one fix-worker pass against a finding.

oneOf.description: The worker finished and handed off to the next.
The validator verified resolution and closed the finding.
The validator rejected the fix; the bolt budget was spent.

FeedbackItem.description: A single feedback item as projected onto the wire.

closed_by.description: Unit slug that certified closure, or `None` while open.

closure_reply_unread.description: Whether the closure reply is unacknowledged.

scope.description: Whether the item is run- or station-scope.

source_ref.description: Back-reference to the origin artifact, or null.

FeedbackItem.title: FeedbackItem

station.description: The station.

close_as_answered.description: If `true`, transitions the parent to `answered` in the same write.

FeedbackStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of a feedback item.

`escalated` is reached when a worker-authored item's fix loop exceeds the
bolt cap without closure — a human-intervention waypoint, not a blocker.
Only `pending` and `fixing` block the checkpoint gate.

A fix has landed, awaiting verification.
Resolved by a reply, no code delta.
Valid but needs no code fix.
Bolt cap exceeded; awaiting a human.

reply_index.description: Zero-based index of the appended reply.

status.description: The parent status after the write.

FeedbackResolution.description:
Routing hint for the manager's feedback resolver.

`None` on the wire means the caller has no preference and the router
defaults to `stage_revisit`.

oneOf.description: Skip the fix loop; answer the question.

FeedbackUpdateRequest.description:
`PUT /api/feedback/:run/:station/:id` request body.

At least one of `status` / `closed_by` / `resolution` must be supplied; an
empty body is a `400`.

closed_by.description: Unit slug certifying closure, if changing.

FeedbackUpdateRequest.title: FeedbackUpdateRequest

updated_fields.description: Frontmatter fields that were actually changed.

VisualReviewAnnotations.description:
The annotation bundle a visual review produces over an output screenshot:
pins on the rendered surface plus free-text comments. This is the FEEDBACK
the operator gives by annotating the OUTPUT (distinct from a pre-build
design direction).

pins.description: Pin annotations on the output screenshot.

VisualReviewPin.description:
A pin dropped on an OUTPUT screenshot during visual review — reuses the
pin annotation model (relative coordinate + note) over an artifact's
rendered screenshot.

note.description: The note attached to the pin.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to the screenshot width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to the screenshot height).

OutputReviewRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /visual-review/:id/annotate` — the operator's
annotations over the output screenshot.

annotations.description: The pin + comment annotations on the output screenshot.

title.description:
Optional title for the produced feedback item (defaults to a
surface-derived label server-side).

OutputReviewRequest.title: OutputReviewRequest

OutputReviewResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /visual-review/:id/annotate` (201 on success).

Echoes the recorded annotation counts and the id of the FEEDBACK item the
annotation produced.

feedback_id.description: The minted `FB-NN` feedback id the annotation produced.

OutputReviewResponse.title: OutputReviewResponse

id.description:
The id of the option to select — must match one of the session's
options.
The selected option id, echoed back.

PickerKind.description: The kind of selection a picker session blocks on.

PickerOption.description: One option in a picker session.

id.description: Canonical id echoed back on selection.

secondary.description: Whether the option is hidden behind a "show all" expansion.

SessionStatus.description: Runtime status spanning every interactive session type.

AuditResult.description:
One objective audit result — a named check with its measured value and a
boolean pass/fail. Audits are the a11y/contrast/touch-target/reduced-motion
checks the headless browser runs against a visual surface.

pass.description: Whether the audit passed its threshold.

value.description: The measured value, rendered as a string (e.g. `4.8:1`, `44px`, `0`).

BenchProof.description:
The benchmark block of a [`Proof`] — populated for bench surfaces
(library / api / data) measured through criterion + a small load harness.

Percentiles are latency in milliseconds; throughput is operations per
second.

samples.description: Number of samples the percentiles were computed over.

Surface.description:
The kind of SURFACE a run delivers — mirrors
[`darkrun_core::domain::Surface`] on the wire, kept local so `darkrun-api`
stays dependency-light.

The surface is the linchpin that routes objective verification: visual
surfaces go through a headless browser, bench surfaces through criterion +
a load harness, terminal surfaces through an output snapshot.

oneOf.description: A reusable code library (criterion benches + load harness).
A network API surface (criterion benches + load harness).
A web UI (headless browser: screenshot + vitals + a11y audits).
A desktop application (headless browser: screenshot + vitals + a11y).
A mobile application (headless browser: screenshot + vitals + a11y).
A data pipeline / dataset (criterion benches + load harness).

WebProof.description:
The web-vitals + audit block of a [`Proof`] — populated for visual surfaces
(web-ui / desktop / mobile) measured through a real headless browser.

screenshot_url.description: URL of the captured screenshot the proof was measured against.

vitals.description:
Web vitals keyed by metric name (`lcp`, `fcp`, `cls`, `ttfb`, `inp`),
in their native units (ms, or unitless for CLS).

Proof.description:
The PROOF payload — a run's attached objective evidence.

Surface-tagged: the [`web`](Proof::web) block is populated for visual
surfaces, the [`bench`](Proof::bench) block for bench surfaces, and a
terminal surface carries neither (its snapshot rides on a `web`-less proof,
or a `web` block carrying only a `screenshot_url`).

surface.description: The surface this proof measures — routes which block is authoritative.

Proof.title: Proof

ProofAttachRequest.description: Request body for attaching a run's [`Proof`] — `POST /api/proof/:run`.

station.description: The station the proof was measured at, if scoped.

ProofAttachRequest.title: ProofAttachRequest

ProofAttachResponse.description: Response body for attaching a run's proof (201 on success).

block_matches_surface.description: Whether the proof's block matched its surface's verification route.

run.description: The run the proof was attached to.

ProofGetResponse.description: Response body for reading a run's attached proof — `GET /api/proof/:run`.

run.description: The run the proof belongs to.

station.description: The station the proof was measured at, if scoped.

ProofSessionPayload.description:
The proof-session payload (`session_type = "proof"`) — the Prove station's
objective evidence surfaced as a session.

Carries the run's [`Proof`] (surface-tagged web vitals + audits, or bench
percentiles + throughput) so the desktop app can render the NUMBERS.

run_slug.description: The run slug the proof belongs to.

station.description: The station the proof was measured at, if scoped.

ProofSessionPayload.title: ProofSessionPayload

InlineComment.description: An inline comment anchored to a span of text in a reviewed artifact.

location.description: Artifact path the comment was made on, relative to the run root.

paragraph.description: Zero-based paragraph index inside the reviewed artifact.

selected_text.description: The exact highlighted text the comment anchors to.

QuestionAnnotations.description: Annotation bundle attached to a question answer.

QuestionPin.description:
A per-image pin annotation on a question session — like [`Pin`] but tagged
with which reference image it sits on.

image_index.description: Index into the question's `image_urls[]` this pin sits on.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to image width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to image height).

QuestionScreenshotAnnotation.description:
One reviewer annotation pass over a question reference image — a comment
plus a screenshot of the captured surface.

comment.description: Reviewer's note on this annotation pass.

image_index.description: Index into the question's `image_urls[]` this was captured on.

screenshot_data_url.description: `data:image/png;base64,...` URL of the captured surface + strokes.

QuestionAnswerRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /question/:id/answer`.

A visual question is a single prompt with a list of options; the answer is
the set of selected option ids (one for single-select, many for
multi-select) plus an optional free-text elaboration.

annotations.description:
Optional annotation bundle (pins / screenshots over the reference
images).

selected.description: The option ids the operator selected.

QuestionAnswerRequest.title: QuestionAnswerRequest

QuestionAnswer.description:
The operator's answer to a visual question — the chosen option ids plus an
optional free-text note.

selected.description:
The option ids the operator selected (one for single-select, many for
multi-select).

QuestionAnswer.description:
The operator's answer to a visual question — the chosen option ids plus an
optional free-text note.

selected.description:
The option ids the operator selected (one for single-select, many for
multi-select).

QuestionOption.description:
One selectable option in a visual question — a single choice the operator
can pick, optionally backed by a generated image and a description.

description.description: Optional longer description rendered under the label.

id.description: Canonical id echoed back in the answer's `selected[]`.

image_url.description:
Optional generated-image URL (a mockup / design option to pick among).
When `image_url_light` is also set, this is the **dark-theme** variant.

image_url_light.description:
Optional **light-theme** variant of `image_url`. When present, the option
is multi-theme: the desktop/web view shows whichever matches the active
theme. Absent → `image_url` is theme-neutral and always shown.

SessionStatus.description: Runtime status spanning every interactive session type.

QuestionSessionPayload.description:
The question-session payload (`session_type = "question"`) — a VISUAL
QUESTION the agent poses mid-run: a prompt plus a list of options (each an
optionally-image-backed choice) the operator picks among.

image_urls.description:
Reference image URLs the question annotates (e.g. the surface under
discussion), distinct from per-option images.

multi_select.description: Whether more than one option may be selected.

options.description: The selectable options (image-backed design choices, or plain choices).

prompt.description: The question prompt rendered above the options.

run_slug.description:
The run this question belongs to — lets the engine persist it under the
run and surface it on the run's desktop channel (mirrors Direction /
Picker, which already carry this).

QuestionSessionPayload.title: QuestionSessionPayload

FeedbackSummary.description: Per-status counts of feedback items for the active station.

addressed.description: Items with a landed fix awaiting verification.

ReviewCurrentStation.description: A station entry in the run-state summary.

label.description:
The domain-facing display label shown over the fixed position (legal →
`Intake`). `None` → the UI shows the position name. Display-only.

name.description: The station name (the fixed FSSBPH position slug, e.g. `frame`).

visits.description: The number of times the station has been visited.

ReviewCurrentUnit.description: A unit entry in the run-state summary.

station.description: The active station, or null when between stations.

station_label.description:
The active station's domain label, when it differs from the position
slug. Display-only; `None` → show the position name.

surface.description:
The delivery surface the run was classified into at Shape (`web_ui`,
`library`, …), or null until classified. Routes how Prove/Audit verify.

units.description: The units in the active station.

InlineComment.description: An inline comment anchored to a span of text in a reviewed artifact.

location.description: Artifact path the comment was made on, relative to the run root.

paragraph.description: Zero-based paragraph index inside the reviewed artifact.

selected_text.description: The exact highlighted text the comment anchors to.

Pin.description: A pin annotation dropped on a reviewed surface.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to surface width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to surface height).

ReviewAnnotations.description: Annotations attached to a review decision.

screenshot.description: Base64-encoded PNG of the annotated canvas.

annotations.description: Optional annotations attached to the decision.

decision.description:
Raw decision string — the server canonicalizes to one of the
[`ReviewDecision`] variants.

ReviewDecision.description:
The canonical review decision. Anything other than `approved` is coerced
to `changes_requested` server-side.

feedback.description: The feedback echoed back (empty when none was supplied).

ApproveAction.description: The server-computed Approve button label + consequence.

ApproveActionKind.description: The consequence the Approve button will trigger.

DiscoveredReviewSource.description: The provenance of an auto-discovered review URL.

DiscoveredReviewUrl.description: A delivery PR/MR auto-discovered from a published head ref.

GateType.description:
The kind of Checkpoint gate that opened a review session.

Mirrors [`darkrun_core::domain::CheckpointKind`] on the wire — kept as a
separate type so `darkrun-api` stays dependency-light.

oneOf.description: Hand off to an external review surface.

InlineComment.description: An inline comment anchored to a span of text in a reviewed artifact.

location.description: Artifact path the comment was made on, relative to the run root.

paragraph.description: Zero-based paragraph index inside the reviewed artifact.

selected_text.description: The exact highlighted text the comment anchors to.

KnowledgeFile.description: A named knowledge file surfaced in a review.

MilestoneStatus.description: Status of one milestone in a station's granular progress track.

OutputArtifact.description: One declared output deliverable surfaced in a review.

content.description: Inline content, when small enough to embed.

directory.description: Subdirectory grouping under the station dir.

run_relative_path.description: Original run-dir-relative path, for "declared by" lookups.

station.description: The station the artifact belongs to (empty string = run scope).

OutputArtifactType.description: The render kind of an output artifact.

PendingDecision.description:
A decision the desktop app submitted while no await was open. Drained on the
next `darkrun_await_gate` entry.

Pin.description: A pin annotation dropped on a reviewed surface.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to surface width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to surface height).

PreviousReviewSnapshot.description:
A snapshot of a prior review, attached when the current review follows a
changes-requested decision.

run_raw_content.description: Raw run-document content at the time.

unit_raw_contents.description: Raw unit-document content keyed by unit slug.

ProgressMilestone.description: One ordered milestone in a station's granular progress track.

status.description: Whether this milestone is done, active, or pending.

ReviewAnnotations.description: Annotations attached to a review decision.

screenshot.description: Base64-encoded PNG of the annotated canvas.

RunCurrentState.description: Unified current-state snapshot — "where is this run right now?".

awaiting_merge_into.description: The default branch the work is waiting to land on, when pending seal.

milestones.description: Granular milestone track for the active station.

progress_index.description: Index of the active (first not-done) milestone.

seal_status.description: Terminal seal state, present once every station has merged.

station.description: The active station.

RunPhase.description:
The fixed phase taxonomy a Station walks (mirrors
`darkrun_core::domain::StationPhase`, kept local to stay dependency-light).

oneOf.description:
Audit the output against the spec AND run the quality checks / tests
(the old `Tests` phase folded in here).
Reflect: autonomous retrospective feeding the run-level reflections.

SealStatus.description: Terminal seal state of a run.

oneOf.description: Built and signed, but the branch hasn't landed yet.

SessionStatus.description: Runtime status spanning every interactive session type.

station.description: The station the artifact belongs to.

StationStateInfo.description: Per-station status snapshot surfaced in a review payload.

gate_entered_at.description: When the checkpoint gate was entered.

merged_into_main.description:
Whether the station's work has merged into the run's main line — the
only authoritative predicate; the rest are display shims.

station.description: The station name.

UnitOutputPreview.description: One per-unit output preview entry — one per path a Unit declared.

exists.description: Whether the declared output actually exists on disk.

preview_body.description: Inline preview body (markdown source or raw HTML).

UnitOutputType.description: The render kind of a per-unit output preview.

ReviewSessionPayload.description:
The review-session payload (`session_type = "review"`) — the load-bearing
variant for phase 1.

Opaque parsed artifacts (run/unit/criteria structures built from markdown)
are carried as raw JSON [`Value`]s — a loose-by-design approach for internal
parser output.

ad_hoc.description: Whether this is an ad-hoc (non-gate) review.

approve_action.description: The Approve button's computed label + consequence.

await_active.description:
True when a `darkrun_await_gate` call is currently blocked on this
session (drives the Approve button's enabled state).

await_count.description: Cumulative number of awaits that have run on this session.

gate_context.description: Where in the lifecycle this gate fires.

gate_type.description: The checkpoint gate kind that opened this review.

knowledge_files.description: Knowledge files surfaced in the review.

next_phase.description: The phase that begins after approval.

next_station.description: The station that begins after approval.

other_files.description: Stray station-scope files not declared by any unit.

output_declared_by.description: Inverse of `unit_outputs`: output path -> declaring unit slugs.

pending_decision.description: A decision queued while no await was open.

previous_review.description: The prior review snapshot, on a changes-requested follow-up.

station.description: The station the review was opened against.

station_briefs.description:
Per-station user-facing PRE-execution briefs ("what I'm going to do"),
keyed by station name.

station_elaborations.description: Per-station elaboration narratives, keyed by station name.

station_milestones.description: Per-station milestone tracks, keyed by station name.

station_observations.description: Per-station worker observations, keyed by station name.

station_outcomes.description:
Per-station closing OUTCOMES ("what the station actually produced"),
keyed by station name.

station_states.description:
Per-station status snapshots, in FACTORY ORDER (not alphabetical) — the
desktop renders the station strip in this order, so it's a Vec, not a map.

station_summaries.description: Per-station definition summaries, keyed by station name.

unit_outputs.description: Per-unit output previews, keyed by unit slug.

ReviewSessionPayload.title: ReviewSessionPayload

MilestoneStatus.description: Status of one milestone in a station's granular progress track.

ProgressMilestone.description: One ordered milestone in a station's granular progress track.

status.description: Whether this milestone is done, active, or pending.

The fixed phase taxonomy a Station walks (mirrors
`darkrun_core::domain::StationPhase`, kept local to stay dependency-light).

Audit the output against the spec AND run the quality checks / tests
(the old `Tests` phase folded in here).
Reflect: autonomous retrospective feeding the run-level reflections.

awaiting_merge_into.description: The default branch the work is waiting to land on, when pending seal.

milestones.description: Granular milestone track for the active station.

progress_index.description: Index of the active (first not-done) milestone.

seal_status.description: Terminal seal state, present once every station has merged.

station.description: The active station.

RunCurrentState.title: RunCurrentState

RunDetailStation.description: A station row in a run's detail view.

phase.description: Current phase within the station, if known.

RunDetailUnit.description: A unit row in a run's detail view.

station.description: The station the unit belongs to, if recorded.

StationProgress.description:
Station-progress fraction for a run: how many stations have completed out of
the total the run walks. Surfaced as a small object so the SPA can render a
`3 / 6` chip (or a bar) without recomputing it client-side.

RunDetailPayload.description:
`GET /api/runs/:slug` response body: a single run's expanded view — its
identity, the live position, every station it walks, and the units that
sit on the active station.

active_station.description: The station the run currently sits on.

stations.description: Every station the run walks, in declared order.

units.description: The units on the active station.

RunDetailPayload.title: RunDetailPayload

RunSummary.description:
A compact summary of a single run for the browse list.

Mirrors the engine's `run_list`-style projection: identity, the live
position (`active_station` + `phase`), lifecycle `status`, station
`progress`, and the `started_at` timestamp. `title` is always resolved
(falling back to the slug when no explicit title was set); `phase` is
nullable for a run sitting between stations.

active_station.description: The station the run currently sits on.

author.description:
The run branch's authoring email, when one could be attributed (the
author of the most recent commit on the branch beyond its base). `None`
when the branch carries no run-local commits, or authorship was not
resolved.

authored_by_me.description:
Whether the current git identity authored any commit on the run's
branch — the engine's "Mine" predicate the sidebar filters on. Defaults
to `false` so an older producer (or a non-git project) is treated as
"not mine" rather than failing to decode.

phase.description: The active phase within the active station, if known.

slug.description: The run slug — the stable id used in every run-scoped route.

title.description: Resolved display title (falls back to the slug).

StationProgress.description:
Station-progress fraction for a run: how many stations have completed out of
the total the run walks. Surfaced as a small object so the SPA can render a
`3 / 6` chip (or a bar) without recomputing it client-side.

RunListPayload.description:
`GET /api/runs` response body: every (non-archived) run on the project as a
summary, sorted by slug.

runs.description: The run summaries, sorted by slug.

RunListPayload.title: RunListPayload

RunPhase.description:
The fixed phase taxonomy a Station walks (mirrors
`darkrun_core::domain::StationPhase`, kept local to stay dependency-light).

oneOf.description:
Audit the output against the spec AND run the quality checks / tests
(the old `Tests` phase folded in here).
Reflect: autonomous retrospective feeding the run-level reflections.

RunPhase.title: RunPhase

StationProgress.description:
Station-progress fraction for a run: how many stations have completed out of
the total the run walks. Surfaced as a small object so the SPA can render a
`3 / 6` chip (or a bar) without recomputing it client-side.

RunSummary.description:
A compact summary of a single run for the browse list.

Mirrors the engine's `run_list`-style projection: identity, the live
position (`active_station` + `phase`), lifecycle `status`, station
`progress`, and the `started_at` timestamp. `title` is always resolved
(falling back to the slug when no explicit title was set); `phase` is
nullable for a run sitting between stations.

active_station.description: The station the run currently sits on.

author.description:
The run branch's authoring email, when one could be attributed (the
author of the most recent commit on the branch beyond its base). `None`
when the branch carries no run-local commits, or authorship was not
resolved.

authored_by_me.description:
Whether the current git identity authored any commit on the run's
branch — the engine's "Mine" predicate the sidebar filters on. Defaults
to `false` so an older producer (or a non-git project) is treated as
"not mine" rather than failing to decode.

phase.description: The active phase within the active station, if known.

slug.description: The run slug — the stable id used in every run-scoped route.

title.description: Resolved display title (falls back to the slug).

RunSummary.title: RunSummary

ApproveAction.description: The server-computed Approve button label + consequence.

ApproveActionKind.description: The consequence the Approve button will trigger.

AuditResult.description:
One objective audit result — a named check with its measured value and a
boolean pass/fail. Audits are the a11y/contrast/touch-target/reduced-motion
checks the headless browser runs against a visual surface.

pass.description: Whether the audit passed its threshold.

value.description: The measured value, rendered as a string (e.g. `4.8:1`, `44px`, `0`).

BenchProof.description:
The benchmark block of a [`Proof`] — populated for bench surfaces
(library / api / data) measured through criterion + a small load harness.

Percentiles are latency in milliseconds; throughput is operations per
second.

samples.description: Number of samples the percentiles were computed over.

DirectionAnnotations.description:
Annotations the operator attaches when giving a design direction — pins on
the chosen archetype, an optional captured-screenshot reference, and a list
of free-text comments.

screenshot.description:
Optional reference to a captured screenshot — a `data:image/...;base64`
URL or a server-relative artifact path.

DirectionArchetype.description:
One design ARCHETYPE card in a direction session — a named design direction,
always backed by a generated preview image.

description.description: Description of the design direction this archetype represents.

image_url.description:
Generated preview-image URL for this archetype. When `image_url_light` is
also set, this is the **dark-theme** variant.

image_url_light.description:
Optional **light-theme** variant of `image_url`. When present, the
archetype is multi-theme: the view shows whichever matches the active
theme. Absent → `image_url` is theme-neutral and always shown.

DirectionPin.description: A pin dropped on a direction preview at a relative coordinate.

note.description: The note attached to the pin.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to the preview width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to the preview height).

DirectionSessionPayload.description:
The direction-session payload (`session_type = "direction"`) — a DESIGN
DIRECTION the agent asks for: a prompt plus design archetypes (each an
image-backed direction), the chosen archetype id, and annotations.

annotations.description: Annotations attached to the chosen direction.

archetypes.description: The design archetypes to choose between.

chosen_archetype.description: The id of the archetype the operator chose, once decided.

context.description: Optional markdown preamble above the archetype cards.

prompt.description: The prompt rendered above the archetype cards.

DiscoveredReviewSource.description: The provenance of an auto-discovered review URL.

DiscoveredReviewUrl.description: A delivery PR/MR auto-discovered from a published head ref.

GateType.description:
The kind of Checkpoint gate that opened a review session.

Mirrors [`darkrun_core::domain::CheckpointKind`] on the wire — kept as a
separate type so `darkrun-api` stays dependency-light.

oneOf.description: Hand off to an external review surface.

InlineComment.description: An inline comment anchored to a span of text in a reviewed artifact.

location.description: Artifact path the comment was made on, relative to the run root.

paragraph.description: Zero-based paragraph index inside the reviewed artifact.

selected_text.description: The exact highlighted text the comment anchors to.

KnowledgeFile.description: A named knowledge file surfaced in a review.

MilestoneStatus.description: Status of one milestone in a station's granular progress track.

OutputArtifact.description: One declared output deliverable surfaced in a review.

content.description: Inline content, when small enough to embed.

directory.description: Subdirectory grouping under the station dir.

run_relative_path.description: Original run-dir-relative path, for "declared by" lookups.

station.description: The station the artifact belongs to (empty string = run scope).

OutputArtifactType.description: The render kind of an output artifact.

PendingDecision.description:
A decision the desktop app submitted while no await was open. Drained on the
next `darkrun_await_gate` entry.

PickerKind.description: The kind of selection a picker session blocks on.

PickerOption.description: One option in a picker session.

id.description: Canonical id echoed back on selection.

secondary.description: Whether the option is hidden behind a "show all" expansion.

Pin.description: A pin annotation dropped on a reviewed surface.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to surface width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to surface height).

PreviousReviewSnapshot.description:
A snapshot of a prior review, attached when the current review follows a
changes-requested decision.

run_raw_content.description: Raw run-document content at the time.

unit_raw_contents.description: Raw unit-document content keyed by unit slug.

ProgressMilestone.description: One ordered milestone in a station's granular progress track.

status.description: Whether this milestone is done, active, or pending.

Proof.description:
The PROOF payload — a run's attached objective evidence.

Surface-tagged: the [`web`](Proof::web) block is populated for visual
surfaces, the [`bench`](Proof::bench) block for bench surfaces, and a
terminal surface carries neither (its snapshot rides on a `web`-less proof,
or a `web` block carrying only a `screenshot_url`).

surface.description: The surface this proof measures — routes which block is authoritative.

ProofSessionPayload.description:
The proof-session payload (`session_type = "proof"`) — the Prove station's
objective evidence surfaced as a session.

Carries the run's [`Proof`] (surface-tagged web vitals + audits, or bench
percentiles + throughput) so the desktop app can render the NUMBERS.

run_slug.description: The run slug the proof belongs to.

station.description: The station the proof was measured at, if scoped.

QuestionAnswer.description:
The operator's answer to a visual question — the chosen option ids plus an
optional free-text note.

selected.description:
The option ids the operator selected (one for single-select, many for
multi-select).

QuestionOption.description:
One selectable option in a visual question — a single choice the operator
can pick, optionally backed by a generated image and a description.

description.description: Optional longer description rendered under the label.

id.description: Canonical id echoed back in the answer's `selected[]`.

image_url.description:
Optional generated-image URL (a mockup / design option to pick among).
When `image_url_light` is also set, this is the **dark-theme** variant.

image_url_light.description:
Optional **light-theme** variant of `image_url`. When present, the option
is multi-theme: the desktop/web view shows whichever matches the active
theme. Absent → `image_url` is theme-neutral and always shown.

QuestionSessionPayload.description:
The question-session payload (`session_type = "question"`) — a VISUAL
QUESTION the agent poses mid-run: a prompt plus a list of options (each an
optionally-image-backed choice) the operator picks among.

image_urls.description:
Reference image URLs the question annotates (e.g. the surface under
discussion), distinct from per-option images.

multi_select.description: Whether more than one option may be selected.

options.description: The selectable options (image-backed design choices, or plain choices).

prompt.description: The question prompt rendered above the options.

run_slug.description:
The run this question belongs to — lets the engine persist it under the
run and surface it on the run's desktop channel (mirrors Direction /
Picker, which already carry this).

ReviewAnnotations.description: Annotations attached to a review decision.

screenshot.description: Base64-encoded PNG of the annotated canvas.

ReviewSessionPayload.description:
The review-session payload (`session_type = "review"`) — the load-bearing
variant for phase 1.

Opaque parsed artifacts (run/unit/criteria structures built from markdown)
are carried as raw JSON [`Value`]s — a loose-by-design approach for internal
parser output.

ad_hoc.description: Whether this is an ad-hoc (non-gate) review.

approve_action.description: The Approve button's computed label + consequence.

await_active.description:
True when a `darkrun_await_gate` call is currently blocked on this
session (drives the Approve button's enabled state).

await_count.description: Cumulative number of awaits that have run on this session.

gate_context.description: Where in the lifecycle this gate fires.

gate_type.description: The checkpoint gate kind that opened this review.

knowledge_files.description: Knowledge files surfaced in the review.

next_phase.description: The phase that begins after approval.

next_station.description: The station that begins after approval.

other_files.description: Stray station-scope files not declared by any unit.

output_declared_by.description: Inverse of `unit_outputs`: output path -> declaring unit slugs.

pending_decision.description: A decision queued while no await was open.

previous_review.description: The prior review snapshot, on a changes-requested follow-up.

station.description: The station the review was opened against.

station_briefs.description:
Per-station user-facing PRE-execution briefs ("what I'm going to do"),
keyed by station name.

station_elaborations.description: Per-station elaboration narratives, keyed by station name.

station_milestones.description: Per-station milestone tracks, keyed by station name.

station_observations.description: Per-station worker observations, keyed by station name.

station_outcomes.description:
Per-station closing OUTCOMES ("what the station actually produced"),
keyed by station name.

station_states.description:
Per-station status snapshots, in FACTORY ORDER (not alphabetical) — the
desktop renders the station strip in this order, so it's a Vec, not a map.

station_summaries.description: Per-station definition summaries, keyed by station name.

unit_outputs.description: Per-unit output previews, keyed by unit slug.

RunCurrentState.description: Unified current-state snapshot — "where is this run right now?".

awaiting_merge_into.description: The default branch the work is waiting to land on, when pending seal.

milestones.description: Granular milestone track for the active station.

progress_index.description: Index of the active (first not-done) milestone.

seal_status.description: Terminal seal state, present once every station has merged.

station.description: The active station.

RunPhase.description:
The fixed phase taxonomy a Station walks (mirrors
`darkrun_core::domain::StationPhase`, kept local to stay dependency-light).

oneOf.description:
Audit the output against the spec AND run the quality checks / tests
(the old `Tests` phase folded in here).
Reflect: autonomous retrospective feeding the run-level reflections.

SealStatus.description: Terminal seal state of a run.

oneOf.description: Built and signed, but the branch hasn't landed yet.

SessionStatus.description: Runtime status spanning every interactive session type.

station.description: The station the artifact belongs to.

StationStateInfo.description: Per-station status snapshot surfaced in a review payload.

gate_entered_at.description: When the checkpoint gate was entered.

merged_into_main.description:
Whether the station's work has merged into the run's main line — the
only authoritative predicate; the rest are display shims.

station.description: The station name.

Surface.description:
The kind of SURFACE a run delivers — mirrors
[`darkrun_core::domain::Surface`] on the wire, kept local so `darkrun-api`
stays dependency-light.

The surface is the linchpin that routes objective verification: visual
surfaces go through a headless browser, bench surfaces through criterion +
a load harness, terminal surfaces through an output snapshot.

oneOf.description: A reusable code library (criterion benches + load harness).
A network API surface (criterion benches + load harness).
A web UI (headless browser: screenshot + vitals + a11y audits).
A desktop application (headless browser: screenshot + vitals + a11y).
A mobile application (headless browser: screenshot + vitals + a11y).
A data pipeline / dataset (criterion benches + load harness).

UnitOutputPreview.description: One per-unit output preview entry — one per path a Unit declared.

exists.description: Whether the declared output actually exists on disk.

preview_body.description: Inline preview body (markdown source or raw HTML).

UnitOutputType.description: The render kind of a per-unit output preview.

ViewArtifact.description:
One browsable output artifact in a view session's artifact browser.

The view session is an ARTIFACT BROWSER: it lists the run's output
deliverables so the operator can inspect them. Each entry carries a stable
id, its run-relative path, a render kind, a display label, and optional
fetch / thumbnail URLs.

id.description: Stable id (referenced by an output-annotation review's `artifact_id`).

path.description: Run-relative path of the artifact on disk.

thumbnail_url.description: Optional thumbnail URL (a small preview for the browser grid).

url.description: Optional full fetch URL (session-scoped, already prefixed).

ViewArtifactKind.description: The render kind of one browsable output artifact in a view session.

ViewMode.description: The mode a view session opened in.

ViewScope.description: The scope a view session browses — which slice of the run's outputs it lists.

oneOf.description: Only the outputs of one station.

ViewSessionPayload.description:
The view-session payload (`session_type = "view"`) — a non-blocking ARTIFACT
BROWSER opened by `darkrun_view`.

The centerpiece is [`artifacts`](ViewSessionPayload::artifacts): the list of
output deliverables the operator browses, narrowed by
[`scope`](ViewSessionPayload::scope). The viewer/boot mode + boot fields are
retained for the dev-server boot path.

artifact.description: Optional artifact deep-link (the `id` of the focused artifact).

artifacts.description: The output artifacts available to browse.

scope.description: What slice of the run's outputs this view browses.

station.description: Optional station narrowing.

status.description: Whether the view is open or closed.

ViewStatus.description: The open/closed status of a view session.

VisualReviewAnnotations.description:
The annotation bundle a visual review produces over an output screenshot:
pins on the rendered surface plus free-text comments. This is the FEEDBACK
the operator gives by annotating the OUTPUT (distinct from a pre-build
design direction).

pins.description: Pin annotations on the output screenshot.

VisualReviewPin.description:
A pin dropped on an OUTPUT screenshot during visual review — reuses the
pin annotation model (relative coordinate + note) over an artifact's
rendered screenshot.

note.description: The note attached to the pin.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to the screenshot width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to the screenshot height).

VisualReviewSessionPayload.description:
The visual-review session payload (`session_type = "visual_review"`) — a
VISUAL REVIEW of an OUTPUT screenshot.

The operator annotates the rendered screenshot of one of the run's output
artifacts (pins + comments over the captured surface), producing feedback.
Reuses the pin annotation model from the design-direction flow, but points
at a *produced* artifact rather than a pre-build design preview.

annotations.description: The annotations the operator produced, once submitted.

artifact_id.description: The id of the [`ViewArtifact`] being annotated.

artifact_path.description: Run-relative path of the artifact being annotated.

prompt.description: Optional prompt rendered above the screenshot.

run_slug.description: The run slug whose output is under review.

screenshot_url.description: URL of the output screenshot the operator annotates.

station.description: The station that produced the output, if scoped.

WebProof.description:
The web-vitals + audit block of a [`Proof`] — populated for visual surfaces
(web-ui / desktop / mobile) measured through a real headless browser.

screenshot_url.description: URL of the captured screenshot the proof was measured against.

vitals.description:
Web vitals keyed by metric name (`lcp`, `fcp`, `cls`, `ttfb`, `inp`),
in their native units (ms, or unitless for CLS).

SessionPayload.description:
The session payload returned by `GET /api/session/:id`, discriminated on
`session_type`.

The `Review` variant is intentionally the largest (it is the load-bearing
phase-1 payload); the size disparity is accepted to keep the public
constructor ergonomic for every caller.

oneOf.description: A visual review of an output screenshot (pin annotations -> feedback).
Objective evidence from the Prove station.

SessionPayload.title: SessionPayload

ValidationIssue.description: A structural validation issue surfaced on the wire.

path.description: JSON pointer-ish path to the offending field.

ValidationError.description:
The uniform `400` envelope returned whenever a request body fails schema
validation (including malformed JSON, surfaced as a synthetic issue).

issues.description: The issues that caused the rejection.

ValidationError.title: ValidationError

ViewArtifact.description:
One browsable output artifact in a view session's artifact browser.

The view session is an ARTIFACT BROWSER: it lists the run's output
deliverables so the operator can inspect them. Each entry carries a stable
id, its run-relative path, a render kind, a display label, and optional
fetch / thumbnail URLs.

id.description: Stable id (referenced by an output-annotation review's `artifact_id`).

path.description: Run-relative path of the artifact on disk.

thumbnail_url.description: Optional thumbnail URL (a small preview for the browser grid).

url.description: Optional full fetch URL (session-scoped, already prefixed).

ViewArtifactKind.description: The render kind of one browsable output artifact in a view session.

ViewMode.description: The mode a view session opened in.

ViewScope.description: The scope a view session browses — which slice of the run's outputs it lists.

oneOf.description: Only the outputs of one station.

ViewStatus.description: The open/closed status of a view session.

ViewSessionPayload.description:
The view-session payload (`session_type = "view"`) — a non-blocking ARTIFACT
BROWSER opened by `darkrun_view`.

The centerpiece is [`artifacts`](ViewSessionPayload::artifacts): the list of
output deliverables the operator browses, narrowed by
[`scope`](ViewSessionPayload::scope). The viewer/boot mode + boot fields are
retained for the dev-server boot path.

artifact.description: Optional artifact deep-link (the `id` of the focused artifact).

artifacts.description: The output artifacts available to browse.

scope.description: What slice of the run's outputs this view browses.

station.description: Optional station narrowing.

status.description: Whether the view is open or closed.

ViewSessionPayload.title: ViewSessionPayload

SessionStatus.description: Runtime status spanning every interactive session type.

VisualReviewAnnotations.description:
The annotation bundle a visual review produces over an output screenshot:
pins on the rendered surface plus free-text comments. This is the FEEDBACK
the operator gives by annotating the OUTPUT (distinct from a pre-build
design direction).

pins.description: Pin annotations on the output screenshot.

VisualReviewPin.description:
A pin dropped on an OUTPUT screenshot during visual review — reuses the
pin annotation model (relative coordinate + note) over an artifact's
rendered screenshot.

note.description: The note attached to the pin.

x.description: X coordinate (0..1 relative to the screenshot width).

y.description: Y coordinate (0..1 relative to the screenshot height).

VisualReviewSessionPayload.description:
The visual-review session payload (`session_type = "visual_review"`) — a
VISUAL REVIEW of an OUTPUT screenshot.

The operator annotates the rendered screenshot of one of the run's output
artifacts (pins + comments over the captured surface), producing feedback.
Reuses the pin annotation model from the design-direction flow, but points
at a *produced* artifact rather than a pre-build design preview.

annotations.description: The annotations the operator produced, once submitted.

artifact_id.description: The id of the [`ViewArtifact`] being annotated.

artifact_path.description: Run-relative path of the artifact being annotated.

prompt.description: Optional prompt rendered above the screenshot.

run_slug.description: The run slug whose output is under review.

screenshot_url.description: URL of the output screenshot the operator annotates.

station.description: The station that produced the output, if scoped.

VisualReviewSessionPayload.title: VisualReviewSessionPayload

info.description: The darkrun software-factory HTTP + WebSocket wire contract.
info.title: darkrun API

post.summary: Wake signal: walk the run past a user checkpoint on the next tick.

get.summary: List feedback items for a run's station.

post.summary: Create a new feedback item in a run's station.

delete.summary: Delete a feedback item (blocks open items via 409).

put.summary: Update status or closed_by on a feedback item.

post.summary: Append a reply to a feedback thread; optionally close as answered.

get.summary: Return a run's attached objective-evidence proof.

post.summary: Attach an objective-evidence proof to a run.

get.summary: Compact run-state summary: active station, units, feedback counts.
List the project's runs as compact summaries, sorted by slug.
Return a run's detail: stations, units on the active station, and phase.
Return session JSON for the desktop app to render.

head.summary: Client presence ping. 200 if the session exists, 404 otherwise.

post.summary: Choose a design archetype and annotate it (the design direction decision).

get.summary: Readiness probe. 200 once listening, 503 while starting.

post.summary: Choose an option in a blocking picker session.
Submit the answer (selected option ids + optional text) to a visual question.
Annotate an output screenshot (pins + comments) -> feedback.
